children.heading: Procurement authority
children.label: a Procurement authority
children.text: Subject to the authority of the Secretary, the Chief Operating Officer of a PBO may exercise the authority of the Secretary to procure property and services in the performance of functions managed by the PBO. For the purposes of this section, the term “PBO” includes the Chief Operating Officer of the PBO and any employee of the PBO exercising procurement authority under the preceding sentence.
enter into contracts to carry out the functions set forth in ;section 1018(b)(2) of this title
obtain the services of experts and consultants without regard to and set pay in accordance with such section; andsection 3109 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, utilize procurement systems that streamline operations, improve internal controls, and enhance management; and

children.label: B
children.text: assess the efficiency of such systems and assess such systems’ ability to meet PBO requirements.

children.heading: Performance-based servicing contracts
children.label: 1 Performance-based servicing contracts
children.text: The Chief Operating Officer shall, to the extent practicable, maximize the use of performance-based servicing contracts, consistent with guidelines for such contracts published by the Office of Federal Procurement Policy, to achieve cost savings and improve service.

children.heading: Fee for service arrangements
children.label: 2 Fee for service arrangements
children.text: The Chief Operating Officer shall, when appropriate and consistent with the purposes of the PBO, acquire services related to the functions set forth in from any entity that has the capability and capacity to meet the requirements set by the PBO. The Chief Operating Officer is authorized to pay fees that are equivalent to those paid by other entities to an organization that provides services that meet the requirements of the PBO, as determined by the Chief Operating Officer.section 1018(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The PBO may use a two-phase process for selecting a source for a procurement of property or services.

children.label: i
children.text: A general description of the scope or purpose of the procurement that provides sufficient information on the scope or purpose for sources to make informed business decisions regarding whether to participate in the procurement.

children.label: ii
children.text: A description of the basis on which potential sources are to be selected to submit offers in the second phase.

children.label: iii
children.text: A description of the information that is to be required under subparagraph (B).

children.label: iv
children.text: Any additional information that the contracting officer determines appropriate.

children.heading: Information submitted by offerors
children.label: B Information submitted by offerors
children.text: Each offeror for the procurement shall submit basic information, such as information on the offeror’s qualifications, the proposed conceptual approach, costs likely to be associated with the proposed conceptual approach, and past performance of the offeror, together with any additional information that is requested by the contracting officer.

children.heading: Selection for second phase
children.label: C Selection for second phase
children.text: The contracting officer shall select the offerors that are to be eligible to participate in the second phase of the process. The contracting officer shall limit the number of the selected offerors to the number of sources that the contracting officer determines is appropriate and in the best interests of the Federal Government.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The contracting officer shall conduct the second phase of the source selection process in accordance with sections 3306(a) to (e) and 3308, chapter 37, and .section 4702 of title 41

children.heading: Eligible participants
children.label: B Eligible participants
children.text: Only the sources selected in the first phase of the process shall be eligible to participate in the second phase.

children.heading: Single or multiple procurements
children.label: C Single or multiple procurements
children.text: The second phase may include a single procurement or multiple procurements within the scope, or for the purpose, described in the notice pursuant to paragraph (2)(A).

children.heading: Procedures considered competitive
children.label: 4 Procedures considered competitive
children.text: The procedures used for selecting a source for a procurement under this subsection shall be considered competitive procedures for all purposes.

children.heading: Use of simplified procedures for commercial products and commercial services
children.label: e Use of simplified procedures for commercial products and commercial services
children.text: Whenever the PBO anticipates that commercial products or commercial services will be offered for a procurement, the PBO may use (consistent with the special rules for commercial products and commercial services) the special simplified procedures for the procurement without regard to any dollar limitation otherwise applicable to the use of those procedures.

children.heading: Flexible wait periods and deadlines for submission of offers of noncommercial products and services
children.label: f Flexible wait periods and deadlines for submission of offers of noncommercial products and services
A
children.text: apply a shorter waiting period for the issuance of a solicitation after the publication of a notice under than is required under subsection (a)(3)(A) of such section; andsection 1708 of title 41

children.label: B
children.text: notwithstanding subsection (a)(3) of such section, establish any deadline for the submission of bids or proposals that affords potential offerors a reasonable opportunity to respond to the solicitation.

children.heading: Inapplicability to commercial products and services
children.label: 2 Inapplicability to commercial products and services
children.text: Paragraph (1) does not apply to a procurement of a commercial product or a commercial service.

children.heading: Consistency with applicable international agreements
children.label: 3 Consistency with applicable international agreements
children.text: If an international agreement is applicable to the procurement, any exercise of authority under paragraph (1) shall be consistent with the international agreement.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The PBO may satisfy the requirements of the PBO for a system incrementally by carrying out successive procurements of modules of the system. In doing so, the PBO may use procedures authorized under this subsection to procure any such module after the first module.

children.heading: Utility requirement
children.label: 2 Utility requirement
children.text: A module may not be procured for a system under this subsection unless the module is useful independently of the other modules or useful in combination with another module previously procured for the system.

children.label: A
children.text: competitive procedures were used for awarding the contract for the procurement of the first module for the system; and

children.label: i
children.text: a general description of the entire system that was sufficient to provide potential offerors with reasonable notice of the general scope of future modules;

children.label: ii
children.text: other information sufficient for potential offerors to make informed business judgments regarding whether to submit offers for the contract for the first module; and

children.label: iii
children.text: a statement that procedures authorized under this subsection could be used for awarding subsequent contracts for the procurement of additional modules for the system.

children.heading: Single-source basis
children.label: A Single-source basis
children.text: Award of the contract on a single-source basis to a contractor who was awarded a contract for a module previously procured for the system under competitive procedures or procedures authorized under subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: a contractor who was awarded a contract for a module previously procured for the system after having been selected for award of the contract under this subparagraph or other competitive procedures; and

children.label: ii
children.text: at least one other offeror that submitted an offer for a module previously procured for the system and is expected, on the basis of the offer for the previously procured module, to submit a competitive offer for the additional module.

children.heading: Other
children.label: C Other
children.text: Award of the contract under any other procedure authorized by law.

children.heading: Publication
children.label: A Publication
children.text: Not less than 30 days before issuing a solicitation for offers for a contract for a module for a system under procedures authorized under subparagraph (A) or (B) of paragraph (4), the PBO shall publish in the Commerce Business Daily a notice of the intent to use such procedures to enter into the contract.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: Publication of a notice is not required under this paragraph with respect to a use of procedures authorized under paragraph (4) if the contractor referred to in that subparagraph (who is to be solicited to submit an offer) has previously provided a module for the system under a contract that contained cost, schedule, and performance goals and the contractor met those goals.

children.heading: Content of notice
children.label: C Content of notice
children.text: A notice published under subparagraph (A) with respect to a use of procedures described in paragraph (4) shall contain the information required under , other than paragraph (4) of such section, and shall invite the submission of any assertion that the use of the procedures for the procurement involved is not in the best interest of the Federal Government together with information supporting the assertion.section 1708(c) of title 41

children.heading: Documentation
children.label: 6 Documentation
children.text: The basis for an award of a contract under this subsection shall be documented. However, a justification pursuant to or is not required.section 3304(e) of title 41section 637(h) of title 15

children.heading: Simplified source-selection procedures
children.label: 7 Simplified source-selection procedures
children.text: The PBO may award a contract under any other simplified procedures prescribed by the PBO for the selection of sources for the procurement of modules for a system, after the first module, that are not to be procured under a contract awarded on a single-source basis.

children.heading: Use of simplified procedures for small business set-asides for services other than commercial services
children.label: h Use of simplified procedures for small business set-asides for services other than commercial services
A
children.text: the procurement is in an amount not greater than $1,000,000;

children.label: B
children.text: the procurement is conducted as a small business set-aside pursuant to ; andsection 644(a) of title 15

children.label: C
children.text: the price charged for supplies associated with the services procured are items of supply expected to be less than 20 percent of the total contract price.

children.label: A
children.text: an award of a contract on a single-source basis; or

children.label: B
children.text: a contract for construction.

children.heading: Guidance for use of authority
children.label: i Guidance for use of authority

children.heading: Issuance by PBO
children.label: 1 Issuance by PBO
children.text: The Chief Operating Officer of the PBO, in consultation with the Administrator for Federal Procurement Policy, shall issue guidance for the use by PBO personnel of the authority provided in this section.

children.heading: Guidance from OFPP
children.label: 2 Guidance from OFPP
children.text: As part of the consultation required under paragraph (1), the Administrator for Federal Procurement Policy shall provide the PBO with guidance that is designed to ensure, to the maximum extent practicable, that the authority under this section is exercised by the PBO in a manner that is consistent with the exercise of the authority by the heads of the other performance-based organizations.

children.heading: Compliance with OFPP guidance
children.label: 3 Compliance with OFPP guidance
children.text: The head of the PBO shall ensure that the procurements of the PBO under this section are carried out in a manner that is consistent with the guidance provided for the PBO under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Limitation on multiagency contracting
children.label: j Limitation on multiagency contracting
children.text: No department or agency of the Federal Government may purchase property or services under contracts entered into or administered by a PBO under this section unless the purchase is approved in advance by the senior procurement official of that department or agency who is responsible for purchasing by the department or agency.

children.heading: Laws not affected
children.label: k Laws not affected
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to waive laws for the enforcement of civil rights or for the establishment and enforcement of labor standards that are applicable to contracts of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Commercial product
children.label: 1 Commercial product
children.text: The term “commercial product” has the meaning given the term in .section 103 of title 41

children.heading: Commercial service
children.label: 2 Commercial service
children.text: The term “commercial service” has the meaning given the term in .section 103a of title 41

children.heading: Competitive procedures
children.label: 3 Competitive procedures
children.text: The term “competitive procedures” has the meaning given the term in .section 152 of title 41

children.heading: Single-source basis
children.label: 4 Single-source basis
children.text: The term “single-source basis”, with respect to an award of a contract, means that the contract is awarded to a source after soliciting an offer or offers from, and negotiating with, only such source (although such source is not the only source in the marketplace capable of meeting the need) because such source is the most advantageous source for purposes of the award.

children.heading: Special rules for commercial products and commercial services
children.label: 5 Special rules for commercial products and commercial services
children.text: The term “special rules for commercial products and commercial services” means the regulations set forth in the Federal Acquisition Regulation pursuant to sections 1901 and 3305(a) of title 41.

children.heading: Special simplified procedures
children.label: 6 Special simplified procedures
children.text: The term “special simplified procedures” means the procedures applicable to purchases of property and services for amounts not greater than the simplified acquisition threshold that are set forth in the Federal Acquisition Regulation pursuant to sections 1901(a)(1) and 3305(a)(1) of title 41.
